goal: Make /app/** read as one coherent Orbit product workspace with a single active demo workspace identity, outcome-oriented nav labels, a primary add-source CTA leading to /app/contacts/new, compact runtime/demo status, and desktop/mobile evidence without overlap or empty labels.

success_criteria.description: GET /app returns HTML containing one shared account summary block (displayName, initials, working context, source posture) that is also rendered by /app/profile, /app/contacts, /app/events, /app/followups, /app/chat, /app/dashboard, and /app/agent — all 8 routes match the same single account identity string; desktop (>=1024px) and mobile (375px) snapshots stored under harness-state/runs/<run-id>/screens/ confirm no overlapping nav chips and no empty nav labels.

Bottom navigation on /app exposes outcome-oriented labels (Relationship cockpit, People, Events, Follow-ups, Conversations, Relationship health, Next moves) instead of internal module names; labels are non-empty and do not truncate at 375px viewport width.

Primary CTA in the app shell is labelled to match its destination (e.g. 'Add a relationship source') and its href resolves to /app/contacts/new; clicking it from /app produces a 200 with the contacts/new composition.

Runtime/demo status is rendered as a compact badge or details disclosure that does not occupy the primary viewport on /app at desktop (>=1024px) or mobile (375px); the primary headline and primary CTA remain the first paint focus.

tests/ui/app-shell.test.tsx and tests/pages/app-page.test.tsx assert the single workspace identity, the outcome-oriented nav labels, the primary CTA target, the compact runtime status, and the mock-to-live replacement doc at shared/ui/app-shell/LIVE_IMPLEMENTATION.md; npm test passes.

out_of_scope: Adding any new backend capability or mock service behavior
Rewriting /app/** page content beyond shell composition and the home primary CTA
Changing the underlying mock service contracts or API envelopes
Introducing any auth, session, or live data source integration
Editing pages or components outside the declared file boundary
Adding new /app/** routes or removing existing /app/** routes
